chapter_context.summary: Jephthah is called from exile; his victory over the Ammonites and his tragic vow.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jephthah Recalled from Exile (11:1-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Judges.
This section focuses on Jephthah's Diplomacy (11:12-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Judges.
This section focuses on Victory and the Vow (11:29-40), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Judges.

key_themes.title: The Cycle of Sin
key_themes.description: The recurring pattern of disobedience, judgment, and rescue.

key_themes.title: Deliverance
key_themes.description: God raising up leaders to save His people despite their failures.

devotional.question: How can we recognize the 'idols' in our culture that lead us away from God?
What does the life of Samson teach us about the danger of wasting God-given potential?

cultural_historical.title: The Period of the Judges
cultural_historical.text: This era is marked by moral decay and the absence of a central king, leading to 'every man doing that which was right in his own eyes.'
